Node.js Basics, Part 10: Setting Up a Firebase Realtime Database
We've already created a project, so next up is setting up Firebase's Realtime Database.
First we need to register an app. Click the code icon on your project.
Give the app a name that's easy for you to recognize, then create it (remember not to check the hosting option).
Next you'll get this project's own config key — copy it down (the Firebase SDK).

<script>

  var firebaseConfig = {
    apiKey: "AIzaSyAd3vTBFRS7o3NuyEubF7JaF-Xc1U7EoAQ",
    authDomain: "testproject-b5784.firebaseapp.com",
    databaseURL: "https://testproject-b5784.firebaseio.com",
    projectId: "testproject-b5784",
    storageBucket: "",
    messagingSenderId: "120474895761",
    appId: "1:120474895761:web:33b6ff0885191e8d"
  };
  // Initialize Firebase
  firebase.initializeApp(firebaseConfig);
</script>
is always required and must be listed first

(Please don't use my Firebase SDK here — I'll delete it at any time. I only pasted it for the sake of this post.)
Next, head back to the console home and click
Once you're in the database section, scroll down to find Realtime Database, then click to create the database.

Since this is just for practice, we'll pick "start in test mode."
(You wouldn't pick test mode in a real production environment — keep that in mind
